ar: وعن أبى جحيفة وهب بن عبد الله رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ رأيت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم بمكة وهو بالأبطح في قبة له حمراء من آدم، فخرج بلال بوضوئه، فمن ناضح ونائل، فخرج النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وعليه حلة حمراء، كأني أنظر إلى بياض ساقيه، فتوضأ وأذن بلال، فجعلت أتتبع فاه ههنا وههنا، يقول يمينا وشمالاً‏:‏ حي على الصلاة، حي على الفلاح، ثم ركزت له عنزة، فتقدم فصلى يمر بين يديه الكلب والحمار لا يمنع‏.((متفق عليه))

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Juhaifah Wahb bin 'Abdullah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) by Al-Abtah valley in Makkah, in a red tent made from tanned skin. Bilal brought him ablution water. Then the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) came out wearing a red mantle; and I can still remember looking at whiteness of his shanks. So he made his ablution, and Bilal (ﷺ) pronounced the call for prayer (Adhan). I kept following the movement of his (Bilal's) face to the right and to the left when he recited: 'Come to the prayer; come to the success.' Then a spear was fixed (as a Sutrah) in front of Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) who then stepped forward and led the prayer. Dogs and donkeys passed in front of him (beyond the spear) and no one prevented them from doing so..
iwh_en: Abu Juhaifah Wahb bin 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i saw the Prophet ﷺ by Al-Abtah valley in Makkah, in a red tent made from tanned skin. Bilal brought him ablution water. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out wearing a red mantle; and I can still remember looking at whiteness of his shanks. So he made his ablution, and Bilal (ﷺ) pronounced the call for prayer (Adhan). I kept following the movement of his (Bilal's) face to the right and to the left when he recited: 'Come to the prayer; come to the success.' Then a spear was fixed (as a Sutrah) in front of Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) who then stepped forward and led the prayer. Dogs and donkeys passed in front of him (beyond the spear) and no one prevented them from doing so..
iwh_id: Abu Juhaifah Wahb bin 'Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i melihat Nabi ﷺ by Al-Abtah valley in Makkah, di red tent membuat from tanned skin. Bilal membawa him wudhu water. Kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ datang out mengenakan a red mantle; dan aku can still remember looking at whiteness of his shanks. Jadi he membuat his wudhu, and Bilal (ﷺ) pronounced the call for shalat (Adhan). I menjaga following the movement of his (Bilal's) face kepada kanan dan kepada meninggalkan ketika ia recited: 'Come kepada shalat; come kepada success.' Kemudian a spear was fixed (as a Sutrah) in front of Rasulullah ﷺ yang kemudian stepped forward dan led the shalat. Dogs dan donkeys passed in front of him (beyond the spear) and no one prevented them from doing jadi..

ar:
وعن أبى سعيد عمرو بن حريث رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ كأني انظر إلى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وعليه عمامة سوداء، قد أرخى طرفيها بين كتفيه‏.‏‏‏((رواه مسلم))‏
‏ وفى رواية له ‏:‏ أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم خطب الناس، وعليه عمامة سوداء‏.

ar:
وعن المغيرة بن شعبة رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ كنت مع رسول الله  صلى الله عليه وسلم ذات ليلة في مسير، فقال لي‏:‏ ‏"‏أمعك ماء‏"‏ ‏؟‏ قلت‏:‏ نعم، فنزل عن راحلته فمشى حتى توارى في سواد الليل ثم جاء فأفرغت علي من الإداواة، فغسل وجهه وعليه جبة من صوف،فلم يستطع أن يخرج ذراعيه منها حتى أخرجهما من أسفل الجبة، فغسل ذراعيه ومسح برأسه، ثم أهويت لأنزع خفيه فقال‏:‏‏"‏دعهما فإني أدخلتهما طاهرتين‏"‏ ومسح عليهما‏.‏((متفق عليه))  
وفى رواية‏:‏وعليه جبة شامية ضيقة الكمين‏.‏ وفى رواية‏:‏ أن هذه القضية كانت في غزوة تبوك‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I was with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) in a journey one night, and he asked me, "Do you have any water with you?" I said, "Yes." So he dismounted from his riding camel and walked away (to answer the call of nature) until he disappeared in the darkness. When he returned, I poured out some water from a vessel and he washed his face. He was wearing a long woolen cloak and could not take out his forearms from his sleeves, so he brought them out from below the cloak and then washed them, and then passed his (wet) hands over his head. I stretched out my hand to take off his Khuff (leather socks), but he said, "Leave them. I put them on after performing Wudu'," and he passed his (wet) hands over them..Another narration is: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was wearing a Syrian cloak with tight sleeves.Another narration is: This incident took place during the battle of Tabuk.
iwh_en: Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in a journey one night, and he asked me, "Do you have any water with you?" I said, "Yes." So he dismounted from his riding camel and walked away (to answer the call of nature) until he disappeared in the darkness. When he returned, I poured out some water from a vessel and he washed his face. He was wearing a long woolen cloak and could not take out his forearms from his sleeves, so he brought them out from below the cloak and then washed them, and then passed his (wet) hands over his head. I stretched out my hand to take off his Khuff (leather socks), but he said, "Leave them. I put them on after performing Wudu'," and he passed his (wet) hands over them..Another narration is: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was wearing a Syrian cloak with tight sleeves.Another narration is: This incident took place during the battle of Tabuk.
iwh_id: Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i was with Rasulullah ﷺ di journey one night, dan ia bertanya me, "Do you have any water with you?" Aku berkata, "Yes." Jadi he dismounted from his riding camel dan berjalan away (to answer the call of nature) hingga he disappeared di darkness. Ketika ia returned, I poured out some water dari vessel dan ia mencuci his face. He was mengenakan a panjang woolen cloak dan tidak bisa mengambil out his forearms from his sleeves, jadi he membawa them out from di bawah the cloak dan kemudian mencuci them, and kemudian passed his (wet) hands over his head. I stretched out my hand to mengambil off his Khuff (khuf), namun ia berkata, "Leave them. I meletakkan them on setelah performing wudhu'," dan ia passed his (wet) hands over them..Another narration is: Rasulullah ﷺ was mengenakan a Syrian cloak with tight sleeves.Another narration is: This incident membawa place selama the battle of Tabuk.

ar: وعن ابن عمر رضى الله عنهما أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من جر ثوبه خيلاء لم ينظر الله إليه يوم القيامة‏"‏ فقال أبو بكر‏:‏ يا رسول الله إن إزاري يسترخي إلى أن أتعاهده، فقال له رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏إنك لست ممن يفعله خيلاء‏"‏‏.‏‏‏((‏رواه البخارى‏)‏‏)‏‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever allows his lower garment to drag out of vanity will find that Allah will not look at him on the Day of Resurrection." On this Abu Bakr (May Allah be pleased with him) replied: "O Messenger of Allah! My lower garment keeps sliding down though I take care to pull it and wrap it." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "You are not of those who do it out of vanity.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "Whoever allows his lower garment to drag out of vanity will find that Allah will not look at him on the Day of Resurrection." On this Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him) replied: "O Messenger of Allah! My lower garment keeps sliding down though I take care to pull it and wrap it." the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "You are not of those who do it out of vanity.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "Barangsiapa yang allows his lower pakaian to drag out of vanity will menemukan that Allah tidak akan look at him on Hari Kebangkitan." On this Abu Bakr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menjawab: "O Rasulullah! My lower pakaian keeps sliding down though I mengambil care to pull it dan wrap it." Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "You bukan of those who do it out of vanity.".

ar: وعن أبى ذر رضى الله عنه عن النبي  صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏ثلاثة لا يكلمهم الله يوم القيامة، ولا ينظر إليهم ، ولا يزكيهم، ولهم عذاب أليم‏"‏ قال‏:‏ فقرأها رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ثلاث مرار‏.‏ قال أبو ذر ‏:‏ خابوا وخسروا ‍ من هم يا رسول  الله ‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏المسبل، والمنان، والمنفق سلعته بالحلف الكاذب‏"‏‏‏‏.‏ وفى رواية له‏:‏ ‏"‏المسبل إزاره‏"‏‏‏.((رواه مسلم))‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Dharr (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "There are three to whom Allah will neither speak on the Day of Resurrection nor will look at them nor purify them (i.e., of their sins), and they will be severely tormented." When he repeated this (statement) thrice, Abu Dharr (May Allah be pleased with him) said: "They are doomed and destroyed! (But) who are they, O Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)?" He said, "One whose lower garment trails, one who boasts of kindness shown to another; and one who promotes sale of his business by taking false oaths.".
iwh_en: Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i heard the Prophet ﷺ saying, "There are three to whom Allah will neither speak on the Day of Resurrection nor will look at them nor purify them (i.e., of their sins), and they will be severely tormented." When he repeated this (statement) thrice, Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "They are doomed and destroyed! (But) who are they, O Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)?" He said, "One whose lower garment trails, one who boasts of kindness shown to another; and one who promotes sale of his business by taking false oaths.".
iwh_id: Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i mendengar Nabi ﷺ saying, "There are three to whom Allah will neither berbicara on Hari Kebangkitan dan tidak will look at them dan tidak mensucikan them (i.e., of their sins), dan mereka will be severely tormented." Ketika ia repeated this (statement) thrice, Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: "They are doomed dan destroyed! (Namun) yang are they, O Rasulullah ﷺ?" He berkata, "One whose lower pakaian trails, one yang boasts of kindness ditunjukkan to another; dan one yang promotes sale of his business by taking false oaths.".

ar: وعن أبى جرى جابر بن سليم رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ رأيت رجلا يصدر الناس عن رأيه ؛ لا يقول شيئاً إلا صدروا عنه؛ قلت‏:‏ من هذا قالوا‏:‏ رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏.‏قلت‏:‏ عليك السلام يا رسول الله - مرتين- قال‏:‏ ‏"‏لا تقل عليك السلام، عليك السلام تحية الموتى -قلت ‏:‏ السلام عليك‏"‏ قال‏:‏ قلت‏:‏ أنت رسول الله ‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏أنا رسول الله إذا أصابك ضر فدعوته كشفه عنك،وإذا أصابك عام سنة فدعوته أنبتها لك، وإذا كنت بأرض قفر أو فلاة، فضلت راحلتك، فدعوته ردها عليك‏"‏ قال‏:‏ قلت‏:‏ اعهد إلى‏.‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏لا تسبن أحداً‏"‏ قال‏:‏ فما سببت بعده حراً، ولا عبداً، ولا بعيراً، ولا شاة ‏"‏ولا تحقرن من المعروف شيئاً، وأن تكلم أخاك وأنت منبسط إليه وجهك؛ إن ذلك من المعروف‏.‏ وارفع إزارك إلى نصف الساق، فإن أبيت فإلى الكعبين، وإياك وإسبال الإزار فإنها من المخيلة وإن الله لا يحب المخيلة، وإن امرؤا شتمك وعيرك، بما يعلم فيك فلا تعيره بما تعلم فيه، فإنما وبال ذلك عليه‏"‏.‏((رواه أبو داود والترمذي))

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Juraiy Jabir bin Sulaim (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I noticed a man whose opinion was followed by everyone, and no one acted contrary to what he said. I asked who he was, and I was informed that he was the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ). I said to him twice: "Alaikas- salam ya Rasul-Allah (May Allah render you safe)." He said, "Do not say: 'Alaikas-salamu.' This is the salutation to the dead (during the time of Jahiliyyah). Say, instead: 'As-salamu 'alaika (May Allah render you safe)."' I asked: "Are you the Messenger of Allah?" He replied, "(Yes) I am the Messenger of Allah, The One Who (Allah) will remove your affliction when you are in trouble and call upon Him, Who will cause food to grow for you when you are famine-stricken and call upon Him, and Who will restore to you your lost riding animal in the desert when you call upon Him." I said to him: "Give me instructions (to act upon)." He (ﷺ) said, "Do not abuse anyone." (Since then I have never abused anyone, neither a freeman, nor a slave, nor a camel, nor a sheep). He (ﷺ) continued, "Do not hold in contempt even an insignificant act of goodness because even talking to your brother with a cheerful countenance is an act of goodness. Hold up your lower garment half way to the leg, and at least above the ankles; for trailing it is arrogance, and Allah dislikes pride. And if a man attributes to you bad things he knows you possess, do not attribute to him bad things that you know he has for he will assume the evil consequences of his abuse.".

This is the salutation to the dead (during the time of Jahiliyyah). Say, instead: 'As-salamu 'alaika (May Allah render you safe)."' I asked: "Are you the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?" He replied, "(Yes) I am the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, The One Who (Allah) will remove your affliction when you are in trouble and call upon Him, Who will cause food to grow for you when you are famine-stricken and call upon Him, and Who will restore to you your lost riding animal in the desert when you call upon Him." I said to him: "Give me instructions (to act upon)." He (ﷺ) said, "Do not abuse anyone." (Since then I have never abused anyone, neither a freeman, nor a slave, nor a camel, nor a sheep). He (ﷺ) continued, "Do not hold in contempt even an insignificant act of goodness because even talking to your brother with a cheerful countenance is an act of goodness. And if a man attributes to you bad things he knows you possess, do not attribute to him bad things that you know he has for he will assume the evil consequences of his abuse.".
iwh_id: Abu Juraiy Jabir bin Sulaim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i noticed seorang laki-laki whose opinion was followed by everyone, and no one acted contrary to what he bersabda. Saya bertanya who he was, dan aku was informed that he was Rasulullah ﷺ. I bersabda to him twice: "Alaikas- salam ya Rasul-Allah (May Allah render you safe)." He bersabda, "Jangan say: 'Alaikas-salamu.' This is the salutation to the dead (selama the time of Jahiliyyah). Say, instead: 'As-salamu 'alaika (May Allah render you safe)."' Saya bertanya: "Are you Rasulullah ﷺ?" Ia menjawab, "(Yes) I am Rasulullah ﷺ, The One Who (Allah) will melepas your affliction when you are in trouble dan call upon Him, Who will cause food to grow for you when you are famine-stricken dan call upon Him, and Who will restore to you your lost riding animal in the desert when you call upon Him." I bersabda to him: "Give me instructions (to act upon)." He (ﷺ) bersabda, "Jangan abuse anyone." (Since kemudian I have never abused anyone, neither a freeman, dan tidak seorang budak, dan tidak a camel, dan tidak a sheep). He (ﷺ) continued, "Jangan hold in contempt even an insignificant act of goodness because even talking to your brother with a cheerful countenance is an act of goodness. Hold up your lower pakaian half way to the leg, and at least di atas the ankles; for trailing it is arrogance, and Allah dislikes pride. And if seorang laki-laki attributes to you buruk hal-hal he knows you possess, jangan attribute to him buruk hal-hal that you mengetahui he has for he will assume the evil consequences of his abuse.".

ar: وعن أبى هريرة رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ بينما رجل يصلى مسبل إزاره، قال له رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏اذهب فتوضأ‏"‏ فذهب فتوضأ، ثم جاء فقال ‏:‏‏"‏ اذهب فتوضأ‏"‏ فقال رجل يا رسول الله ما لك أمرته أن يتوضأ ثم سكت عنه‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏إنه كان يصلى وهو مسبل إزاره، وإن الله لا يقبل صلاة رجل مسبل‏"‏‏.‏((رواه أبو داود))

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said to a man who was performing Salah while his lower garment was trailing, "Go and perform your Wudu' again." The man went and came back having performed it. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said (again), "Go and perform your Wudu'." Someone present said to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ): "O Messenger of Allah! You ask him to perform his Wudu' and then you kept silent (without saying the reason for it)." He (ﷺ) said, "He performed Salah while his lower garment was below his ankles. Allah does not accept the Salah of a man who trails his lower garment.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to a man who was performing Salah while his lower garment was trailing, "Go and perform your Wudu' again." The man went and came back having performed it. the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said (again), "Go and perform your Wudu'." Someone present said to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "O Messenger of Allah! You ask him to perform his Wudu' and then you kept silent (without saying the reason for it)." He (ﷺ) said, "He performed Salah while his lower garment was below his ankles. Allah does not accept the Salah of a man who trails his lower garment.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda to seorang laki-laki who was performing Salah while his lower pakaian was trailing, "Go dan perform your Wudu' again." The man went dan came back having performed it. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda (again), "Go dan perform your Wudu'." Someone present bersabda to Rasulullah ﷺ: "O Rasulullah! You ask him to perform his Wudu' and kemudian you menjaga silent (tanpa saying the reason for it)." He (ﷺ) bersabda, "He performed Salah while his lower pakaian was di bawah his ankles. Allah does not accept the Salah of seorang laki-laki who trails his lower pakaian.".

ar: وعن قيس بن بشر التغلبى قال‏:‏ أخبرني أبى - وكان جليساً لأبى الدرداء-قال‏:‏ كان بدمشق رجل من أصحاب النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يقال له سهل بن الحنظلية، وكان رجلاً متوحداً قلما يجالس الناس، إنما هو في صلاة ، فإذا فرغ فإنما هو تسبيح وتكبير حتى يأتى أهله، فمر بنا ونحن عند أبى الدرداء فقال أبو الدرداء‏:‏ كلمةً تنفعنا ولا تضرك‏.‏ قال‏:‏ بعث رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم سرية فقدمت ، فجاء رجل منهم فجلس في المجلس الذي يجلس فيه رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ، فقال لرجل إلى جنبه‏:‏ لو رأيتنا حين التقينا نحن والعدو،فحمل فلان وطعن، فقال‏:‏ خذها منى،وأنا الغلام الغفارى، كيف ترى في قوله‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ما أراه إلا قد بطل أجره‏.‏ فسمع بذلك آخر فقال‏:‏ ما أرى بذلك بأساً ، فتنازعا حتى سمع رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏سبحان الله‏؟‏لا بأس أن يؤجر ويحمد‏"‏ فرأيت أبا الدرداء سر بذلك، وجعل يرفع رأسه إليه ويقول‏:‏ أنت سمعت ذلك من رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‍‍‍‍‍‍‏؟‏ فيقول‏:‏ نعم‏.‏ فما زال يعيد عليه حتى إني لأقول ليبركن على ركبتيه‏.‏ قال‏:‏ فمر بنا يوماً آخر، فقال له الدرداء‏:‏ كلمة تنفعنا ولا تضرك، قال‏:‏ قال لنا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏المنفق على الخير كالباسط يده بالصدقة ولا يقبضها‏"‏‏.‏ثم مر بنا يوماً آخر، فقال له أبو الدرداء‏:‏ كلمة تنفعنا ولا تضرك، قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏:‏‏"‏نعم الرجل خريم الأسدى ‍‏!‏ لولا طول جمته وإسبال إزاره ‏!‏‏:‏ فبلغ خريما، فجعل، فأخذ شفرة فقطع بها جمته إلى أذنيه ، ورفع إزاره إلى أنصاف ساقية‏.‏ ثم مر بنا يوماً آخر فقال له أبو الدرداء‏:‏ كلمةً تنفعنا ولا تضرك، قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏إنكم قادمون على إخوانكم، فأصلحوا رحالكم، وأصلحوا لباسكم حتى تكونوا كأنكم شامة في الناس، فإن الله لا يحب الفحش ولا التفحش‏"‏‏.‏((رواه أبو داود))

sunnah_ref_en: Qais bin Bishr At-Taghlibi reported that his father, who attended the company of 'Abud-Darda' (May Allah be pleased with him) told him: There was a man in Damascus who was a Companion of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ). He was called Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah. He was a lonesome person and would rarely spend some time in the company of people. He would spend most of his time performing Salah and when he finished, he would engage himself in Tasbih (Subhan-Allah) and Takbir (Allahu Akbar), until he would go home. He passed by us one day when we were sitting with Abud-Darda' (May Allah be pleased with him). The latter said to him: "Tell us something which will benefit for us and will not harm you." He said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) sent a detachment. When they returned, one of them came to the assemblage in which the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was present and said to his neighbor during the conversation: 'I wish you had seen us when we encountered the enemy. So-and-so (a believer) took up his spear, struck and said: Take this from me and I am the Ghifari boy. Now what do you think of this?' The neighbor said: 'I think that he lost his reward because of boasting.' He said: 'I see no harm in it.' They began to exchange arguments till the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) heard them and said, 'Subhan-Allah (Allah is free from every imperfection). He would be rewarded (in the Hereafter) and praised (in this world)'. I noticed that Abud-Darda' (May Allah be pleased with him) felt a great pleasure at this remark and, raising his head began to repeat: "Have you heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say this!" Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah (May Allah be pleased with him) continued responding until I asked Abud-Darda' (May Allah be pleased with him) not to annoy him.Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah (May Allah be pleased with him) happened to pass by us another day and Abud-Darda' said to him: "Tell us something which will benefit us and will not harm you." He said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) told us, 'He who spends to purchase a horse (for Jihad) is like one who extends his hand for spending out of charity without withholding it."'He passed by us another day and Abud-Darda' (May Allah be pleased with him) said to him: "Tell us something which might benefit us, and will not harm you." He said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) once said, 'Khuraim Al-Usaidi is an excellent man were it not for his long hair and his lower garment which is hanging down.' When Khuraim heard about what the Prophet had said about him, he trimmed his long hair up to his ears with a knife and raised his lower garment half way to his shanks."On another occasion he passed by us and Abud-Darda' (May Allah be pleased with him) said to him: "Tell us something that will benefit us and will not harm you." He said that he heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say, while coming back from an expedition: "You are returning to your brothers, so set your saddles and clothes in order so that you look tidy and graceful. Allah hates untidiness.".

iwh_id: Qais bin Bishr At-Taghlibi meriwayatkan bahwa ayahnya, yang attended the company of 'Abud-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) memberitahunya: There was seorang laki-laki in Damascus yang sedang a Companion of Rasulullah ﷺ. He was called Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah. He was a lonesome person dan would rarely spend some time di dekat orang-orang. Beliau spend most of his time performing Salah dan ketika ia finished, ia engage himself in Tasbih (Subhan-Allah) and Takbir (Allahu Akbar), hingga ia go home. He melewati us one day ketika kami sedang duduk with Abud-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu). The latter bersabda kepadanya,: "Tell us sesuatu which will benefit for us dan tidak akan harm you." He berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ sent a detachment. Ketika mereka returned, one of them datang kepada the assemblage in which Rasulullah ﷺ was present dan berkata to his tetangga selama the conversation: 'I wish you had seen us ketika kami encountered the enemy. Jadi-and-jadi (a believer) membawa up his spear, struck dan berkata: Take this from me dan aku am the Ghifari boy. Now what do you berpikir of this?' The tetangga berkata: 'I berpikir that he lost his reward because of boasting.' He berkata: 'I see no harm in it.' They began to exchange arguments till Rasulullah ﷺ mendengar them dan berkata, 'Subhan-Allah (Allah is free from every imperfection). Beliau be rewarded (di Akhirat) and praised (di dunia ini)'. I noticed that Abud-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) felt a besar pleasure at this remark and, raising his head began to repeat: "Have you mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ say this!" Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) continued responding hingga Saya bertanya Abud-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) not to annoy him.Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) happened to pass by us another day and Abud-Darda' bersabda kepadanya,: "Tell us sesuatu which will benefit us dan tidak akan harm you." He berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ memberitahu kami, 'Barangsiapa yang spends to purchase a horse (for Jihad) is like one yang extends his hand for spending out of sedekah tanpa withholding it."'He melewati us another day and Abud-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bersabda kepadanya,: "Tell us sesuatu which might benefit us, and tidak akan harm you." He berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ once berkata, 'Khuraim Al-Usaidi is an excellent man were it not for his panjang hair dan his lower pakaian which is hanging down.' When Khuraim mendengar tentang what Nabi ﷺ had berkata tentang him, he trimmed his panjang hair up to his ears dengan knife dan raised his lower pakaian half way to his shanks."On another occasion he melewati us and Abud-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bersabda kepadanya,: "Tell us sesuatu that will benefit us dan tidak akan harm you." He berkata bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ say, sementara coming back from an expedition: "You are returning to your brothers, jadi set your saddles dan clothes in order sehingga you look tidy dan graceful. Allah hates untidiness.".

ar:
وعن أبى سعيد الخدرى رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏إزارة المسلم إلى نصف الساق، ولا حرج -أو لا جناح- فيما بينه وبين الكعبين، فما كان أسفل من الكعبين فهو في النار، ومن جر إزاره بطرا لم ينظر الله إليه‏"‏‏.((رواه أبو داود))

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The lower garment of a believer should be half way below the knees. He is guilty of no sin if they are up to the ankles. That which is below the ankles is in the Fire (of Hell). Allah will not look at one who allows his lower garment to trail out of vanity..
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The lower garment of a believer should be half way below the knees. He is guilty of no sin if they are up to the ankles. That which is below the ankles is in the Fire (of Hell). Allah will not look at one who allows his lower garment to trail out of vanity..
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The lower pakaian of a believer should be half way di bawah the knees. He is guilty of no sin if they are up to the ankles. That which is di bawah the ankles is in the Fire (of Hell). Allah tidak akan look at one who allows his lower pakaian to trail out of vanity..

ar: وعن ابن عمر رضى الله عنهما قال‏:‏ مررت على رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وفى إزاري استرخاء، فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏يا عبد الله، ارفع إزارك‏"‏ فرفعت ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏زد‏"‏، فزدت، فما زلت أتحراها بعد‏.‏ فقال بعض القوم‏:‏ إلى أين ‏؟‏ فقال‏:‏ إلى أنصاف الساقين‏"‏‏.‏‏((رواه مسلم))‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: My lower garment was trailing as I passed by the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) so he said, "Raise your lower garment, Abdullah." I lifted it up and he told me to raise it higher. I complied with his orders and as I was still trying to find the best place (for it), one of the people asked where it should reach and he (ﷺ) replied, "Half way down the knees.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that my lower garment was trailing as I passed by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ so he said, "Raise your lower garment, Abdullah." I lifted it up and he told me to raise it higher. I complied with his orders and as I was still trying to find the best place (for it), one of the people asked where it should reach and he (ﷺ) replied, "Half way down the knees.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa my lower pakaian was trailing as I melewati Rasulullah ﷺ jadi he bersabda, "Raise your lower pakaian, Abdullah." I lifted it up dan ia told me to raise it higher. I complied with his orders dan as I was still trying to menemukan the best place (for it), one of the orang-orang bertanya where it should reach dan ia (ﷺ) menjawab, "Half way down the knees.".

ar: وعنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ من جر ثوبه خيلاء لم ينظر الله إليه يوم القيامة‏"‏ فقالت أم سلمة‏:‏ فكيف تصنع النساء بذيولهن، قال‏:‏ ‏"‏يرخين شبراً‏"‏ قالت‏:‏ ِإذاً تنكشف أقدامهن‏.‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏فيرخينه ذراعاً لا يزدن‏"‏‏.((رواه أبو داود والترمذي))

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will not look at the one who trails his lower garment out of arrogance." Umm Salamah (May Allah be pleased with her) asked: "What should women do with the hem of their clothes?" He (ﷺ) said, "They might lower them a hand's span." She said: "But their feet would still remain exposed." He said, "Let them lower them equal to arm's length but not more than that.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will not look at the one who trails his lower garment out of arrogance." Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) asked: "What should women do with the hem of their clothes?" He (ﷺ) said, "They might lower them a hand's span." She said: "But their feet would still remain exposed." He said, "Let them lower them equal to arm's length but not more than that.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "On the Hari Kebangkitan, Allah tidak akan look at the one who trails his lower pakaian out of arrogance." Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bertanya: "What should women do with the hem of their clothes?" He (ﷺ) bersabda, "They might lower them a hand's span." She bersabda: "Namun their feet would still remain exposed." He bersabda, "Let them lower them equal to arm's length namun not more than that.".

وعن معاذ بن أنس رضى الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏ من ترك اللباس تواضعاً لله، وهو يقدر عليه، دعاه الله يوم القيامة على رؤوس الخلائق حتى يخيره من أي حلل الإيمان شاء يلبسها‏"‏.((رواه الترمذي))

.

matn_ar_clean:
عن أبى سعيد الخدرى رضى الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم  إذا استجد ثوبا سماه باسمه -عمامة،  أو قميصا، أو رداء -يقول‏:‏ ‏
"‏ اللهم لك الحمد أنت كسوتنيه، أسألك خيره وخير ما صنع له، وأعوذ بك من شره وشر ما صنع له‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود، والترمذى وقال‏:‏حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) wore a new garment, he would name it. For instance, a turban, shirt, or cloak and would supplicate: "Allahumma lakal-hamdu, Anta kasautanihi, as`aluka khairahu wa khaira ma suni`a lahu, wa a`udhu bika min sharrihi wa sharri ma suni`a lahu (O Allah, all the praise is for You that You have given it to me to put on. I ask You its goodness and the goodness of the purpose for which it was made, and I seek Your Protection from its evil and the evil of the purpose for which it was made).''

.
iwh_en:
Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wore a new garment, he would name it. For instance, a turban, shirt, or cloak and would supplicate: "Allahumma lakal-hamdu, Anta kasautanihi, as`aluka khairahu wa khaira ma suni`a lahu, wa a`udhu bika min sharrihi wa sharri ma suni`a lahu (O Allah, all the praise is for You that You have given it to me to put on. I ask You its goodness and the goodness of the purpose for which it was made, and I seek Your Protection from its evil and the evil of the purpose for which it was made).''

.
iwh_id:
Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan:
Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ wore a baru pakaian, ia name it. For instance, a turban, shirt, or cloak dan would supplicate: "Allahumma lakal-hamdu, Anta kasautanihi, as`aluka khairahu wa khaira ma suni`a lahu, wa a`udhu bika min sharrihi wa sharri ma suni`a lahu (Ya Allah, all the praise is for You that You have given it to me to meletakkan on. I ask You its goodness dan the goodness dari purpose for which it was membuat, dan aku mencari Your Protection from its evil dan the evil dari purpose for which it was membuat).''

.
